channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Light sensor is not detecting vehicles problem, 28th April
item.description:
Temp lights were up during the day yesterday and they seem have been left their at 7am. The road seems to be free, no signs of holes in the road or work, just temp lights taking up road users time for what seems like not much reason.
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Light sensor is not detecting vehicles

item.title: TMC - Defects - Footways Slabs problem, 28th April
item.description:
NWD GIVE AND TAKE PLEASE LIFT AND RELAY X1 ROCKING RAISED SLABS X1 600X600X30MM AND X1 900X600X20MM 28MM (FW Rocking/Unstable Slab)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Very large pothole where the road has fallen away. Unclear why potholes close by were filled and this was not. The road as a whole is in a terrible condition due to building and the use of heavy vehicles which has led to road collapse . Other residents and public using this public highway are suffering vehicle damage . There are also overgrown trees which were not adequately cut at the top of the road despite council notice last year. Similarly for trees at the bottom of the lane are also badly overgrown and damaging cars and vans, 28th April
item.description:
Various parts of gravel walk, for trees at top and bottom of road owned by houses abutting Gravel walk who fail to effectively and regularly cut them back. They border a public highway and should be regularly maintained. The residents should not have to constantly report these matters

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Gravel Walk, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Trees blocking highway access, 28th April
item.description:
We understand a noted was served on Richmond last year, the hedge was not properly cut back and its angle is severely restricting access to the lane. Additionally it was only cut up to @5 ft, insufficient for larger cars, vans etc and it is challenging to drive down the public highway. This is reported regularly and should not need to be. It is the responsibility of homeowners abutting a public highway to keep hedges and trees properly and appropriately maintained. This is not the case for houses at the top and bottom of Gravel Walk

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Gravel Walk, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Ash Tree located on private land

item.title: TMC - Defects - Road Markings problem, 28th April
item.description:
Road closure 28 day please refresh give way markings markings approx x33 600x200mm dots plus x2 3 meters lines plus x1 3 meter triangle (Faded Road Markings)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Post or sign leaning slightly (less than 20 degrees) problem, 28th April
item.description:
Damaged sign an post safe at time of. Inspection
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Post or sign leaning slightly (less than 20 degrees)

item.title: Weeds and rubbish, 28th April
item.description:
The pathway between the Methodist Church and courtiers drive is heavily weeded and has litter all along alleyway. Needs a good clear up.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 19, Courtiers Drive, Bishop's Cleeve, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Van been dumped there for about 5 weeks now, 28th April
item.description:
This van has been left here for about 5 weeks no mot and online says been sorn.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 24, Armstrong Road, Stoke Orchard, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: More pitted than the moon, 27th April
item.description:
On the double roundabout, just by the church, is a road worthy of taking the crown of "doctor tyre popper". Although a perfectly good stretch of road in Quedgeley is being replaced, these craters have somehow escaped all requested inspections. As I traverse the counties, I don't believe I've come across another patch of road where you may consider turning around and taking an alternative route, rather than brave the family hatchback to make the crossing, incase a hefty repair bill is to follow. Not even Carterton has a patch of craters to match the murky depths of these impressive wells.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 39, Old Cheltenham Road, Longlevens, Gloucester
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The drain clangs everytime it’s driven over, 27th April
item.description:
There are two drains at top of harp hill outside our bedroom window , both clunk all day all night! Ever since large 20 ton lorry’s were given permission to drive up it! Keeps us and three young children awake all night. Needs fixing asap please
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Harp Hill, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Number 31 Car Parking Issue, 27th April
item.description:
The resident at number 31 has been obstructing parking on an unrestricted road, using graffiti on the road initially marking where people can park either side, using recycling bins and other items to obstruct, and now has had a permanent line drawn- which from what I’m aware of has been done on their own accord and not through the council. When parking outside, if parking ‘too close’ (well before the curb drops) the resident will be verbally abusive and persist that the vehicle needs to be moved.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 31, Strickland Road, Ewens Farm, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Mud and leaves on pavement, slippery when wet., 27th April
item.description:
Mud and leaves on pavement, slippery when wet. Now also has litter dumped there with gutter and curtain pole.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 52, Hewlett Road, Ewens Farm, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dropped Kerb, 27th April
item.description:
During the resurfacing on Swindon Lane, the road has not been replaced to the original height. As a result our dropped kerb is significantly more pronounced. This is noticeably felt within the vehicle when leaving and entering the property. Please note, due to the location of the drive in relation to the road and oncoming traffic, a 90 degree approach and/or exit is infeasible. We will therefore be placing additional stress on car tyres, steering and suspension mechanisms as a direct result of the recent works; this issue was not present prior the resurfacing. I'm hoping the yellow paint (in the images) means this defect has already been spotted by your team and that remedial work is underway.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 6, Swindon Lane, Wymans Brook, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Traffic lights un synchronised, 27th April
item.description:
When turning left from Hyde lane to Cheltenham road, you go in the left hand lane which has its own traffic light. That light has always turned green at the same time as the middle lane (for straight ahead to Southam Lane and turning right towards the Cheltenham racecourse). Recently this has changed, not sure how long maybe a few weeks or longer. Now the light for left turn only stays red throughout the green light to the middle lane and only goes green when the middle lane light turns red.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hyde Lane, Bishop's Cleeve
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Green or amber traffic light is not working

item.title: Flytipped item, 27th April
item.description:
Large baby cradle at the corner of Dunalley Street and St Pauls Road

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 13, St Paul's Road, Marle Hill, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
